steps.title: Plan the composition
steps.description: Lightly mark three spots where mushroom caps will go — vary the heights and don't line them up evenly. Place them so they aren't centered on the page; leave breathing room.

steps.title: Block the caps
steps.description: Sketch each cap as a half-dome at its marked spot. Make them different sizes — a tall one, a medium one, and a small one. Let the smaller caps overlap behind the bigger ones.

steps.title: Block the stems
steps.description: Add a stem under each cap — a simple two-line shape for each. Stems can be thicker near the base and slightly tilted.

steps.title: Refine outlines
steps.description: Trace the caps and stems with a confident single line. Where one mushroom passes in front of another, stop the back one's outline cleanly at the overlap — don't draw through.

steps.title: Add gills underneath
steps.description: Where the underside of a cap is visible, add short radial lines suggesting gills. Don't draw gills on every mushroom — variety reads better.

steps.title: Detail the caps
steps.description: Add a few simple spots or a single curved highlight line to one or two caps. Leave at least one cap mostly empty to keep the composition balanced — busy areas need quiet ones.
